name: Lemon balm
summary: Lemon balm may support gentle calm, stress-related sleep, and relaxed focus with preparation-dependent effects.
description: Lemon balm may support gentle calm, stress-related sleep, and relaxed focus with preparation-dependent effects.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.5: Meta-analysis found lemon balm improved anxiety and depression scores vs placebo; sleep claims should stay secondary unless direct sleep data is added.
claimMap.notes: v9.5: Meta-analysis found lemon balm improved anxiety and depression scores vs placebo; sleep claims should stay secondary unless direct sleep data is added.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Lemon balm dose varies by extract; clinical trials commonly use standardized Melissa officinalis preparations rather than loose tea

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 34449930. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 34449930. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Known allergy to lemon balm or other Lamiaceae/mint-family plants
hypothyroidism or thyroid hormone replacement therapy without clinician review (lemon balm has shown TSH-inhibiting and thyroid-hormone-binding activity in preclinical studies)
planned procedures requiring general anesthesia (discontinue lemon balm several days beforehand due to sedative effects)
May potentiate sedatives or alcohol or benzodiazepines or other CNS depressants through additive drowsiness
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to limited safety data.
